title: prunus domestica f. subsylvestris
intro: Prunus domestica f. subsylvestris, also known as wild plum, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Prunus genus and native to Europe and Western Asia. The bark and leaves of the wild plum have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which help to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have anti-cancer properties, as well as the ability to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Wild plum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and menstrual disorders.
